records.ety: [OE. rescopuen, OF. rescourre, rescurre, rescorre; L. pref. re- re- + excutere to shake or drive out; ex out + quatere to shake. See Qtash to crush, Rercussion.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Rescued (-k?d);p. pr. & vb. n. Rescuing.]

defs: To free or deliver from any confinement, violence, danger, or evil; to liberate from actual restraint; to remove or withdraw from a state of exposure to evil; as, to rescue a prisoner from the enemy; to rescue seamen from destruction.
quotes: Had I been seized by a hungry lion, I would have been a breakfast to the best, Rather than have false Proteus rescue me.

defs: The act of rescuing; deliverance from restraint, violence, or danger; liberation.
quotes: Spur to the rescue of the noble Talbot.

defs: The forcible retaking, or taking away, against law, of things lawfully distrained.
The forcible liberation of a person from an arrest or imprisonment.
The retaking by a party captured of a prize made by the enemy.
quotes: The rescue of a prisoner from the court is punished with perpetual imprisonment and forfeiture of goods.
